webResearchSources.title: AI and Automation: Comprehensive Best Practices and Guide | WorkDone.AI
webResearchSources.supports:
### Defining AI and Automation

Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to technologies that enhance machine capabilities, allowing them to handle complex tasks like problem-solving. Automation, meanwhile

practicalExamples.description: Automate customer inquiry triage in Slack using AI to categorize messages, assign to team members, and log interactions with memory of previous conversations for context-aware responses.
Use AI to analyze CRM data and automatically draft personalized follow-up emails based on past interactions, deal stage, and customer behavior, while remembering communication history.
Implement an AI-powered support ticket system that remembers user issues, categorizes them based on historical patterns, and routes them to appropriate teams with contextual summaries.

implementationSteps.action: Identify a high-volume, repetitive workflow (e.g., Slack inquiries, CRM follow-ups)
implementationSteps.example: A customer service team receives 50+ similar questions daily that can be categorized and responded to using AI with memory of past interactions.

implementationSteps.action: Map out the current manual process and identify decision points where AI can add value
implementationSteps.example: In a support workflow, AI can categorize tickets, suggest responses based on previous resolutions, and escalate complex issues automatically.

implementationSteps.action: Integrate memory layer using tools like vector databases or session context managers
implementationSteps.example: Store customer interaction history in a structured format that AI can reference to provide personalized and context-aware responses.

implementationSteps.action: Add approval steps and quality checks before full automation
implementationSteps.example: AI-drafted responses are reviewed by human agents before being sent, with logs tracking all AI decisions for audit purposes.

implementationSteps.action: Measure business outcomes like time saved, error reduction, and customer satisfaction
implementationSteps.example: Track metrics such as reduced response time by 40%, decreased error rate in ticket categorization, and improved CSAT scores after AI implementation.

commonMistakes.example: Fully automating customer support without review led to inappropriate responses and damaged brand reputation.

commonMistakes.mistake: Ignoring memory and context in AI workflows
commonMistakes.example: Using chatbots that don't remember past interactions resulted in repetitive and frustrating customer experiences.

commonMistakes.mistake: Failing to log and track AI decisions
commonMistakes.example: Not maintaining audit trails of AI actions made it impossible to identify why certain automated decisions were incorrect.
